channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: AriadneMem: Threading the Maze of Lifelong Memory for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.03290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon LLM agents require memory systems that remain accurate under fixed context budgets. However, existing systems struggle with two persistent challenges in long-term dialogue: (i) \textbf{disconnected evidence}, where multi-hop answers requi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, GPT, experiment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Language Model Goal Selection Differs from Humans' in an Open-Ended Task
item.description: arXiv:2603.03295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models (LLMs) get integrated into human decision-making, they are increasingly choosing goals autonomously rather than only completing human-defined ones, assuming they will reflect human preferences. However, human-LLM similarity in...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, GPT, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TTSR: Test-Time Self-Reflection for Continual Reasoning Improvement
item.description: arXiv:2603.03297v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Test-time Training enables model adaptation using only test questions and offers a promising paradigm for improving the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs). However, it faces two major challenges: test questions are often highly difficul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentSelect: Benchmark for Narrative Query-to-Agent Recommendation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03761v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents are rapidly becoming the practical interface for task automation, yet the ecosystem lacks a principled way to choose among an exploding space of deployable configurations. Existing LLM leaderboards and tool/agent benchmarks evaluate compone...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vision, model, API, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RADAR: Learning to Route with Asymmetry-aware DistAnce Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.03388v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent neural solvers have achieved strong performance on vehicle routing problems (VRPs), yet they mainly assume symmetric Euclidean distances, restricting applicability to real-world scenarios. A core challenge is encoding the relational features in...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, embedding, attention, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Improved Sentence Representations using Token Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2603.03389v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Obtaining a single-vector representation from a Large Language Model's (LLM) token-level outputs is a critical step for nearly all sentence-level tasks. However, standard pooling methods like mean or max aggregation treat tokens as an independent set,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, attention, LLM, vector, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Half the Nonlinearity Is Wasted: Measuring and Reallocating the Transformer's MLP Budget
item.description: arXiv:2603.03459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate when transformer MLP nonlinearity is actually necessary. A gate with $d+1$ parameters decides when to replace the full MLP with a linear surrogate. Through systematic investigation across six models (162M-2.8B parameters), two architect...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, context, model, transformer, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: [Re] FairDICE: A Gap Between Theory And Practice
item.description: arXiv:2603.03454v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline Reinforcement Learning (RL) is an emerging field of RL in which policies are learned solely from demonstrations. Within offline RL, some environments involve balancing multiple objectives, but existing multi-objective offline RL algorithms do ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, experiment, study, paper, demonstration | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Specification-Driven Generation and Evaluation of Discrete-Event World Models via the DEVS Formalism
item.description: arXiv:2603.03784v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: World models are essential for planning and evaluation in agentic systems, yet existing approaches lie at two extremes: hand-engineered simulators that offer consistency and reproducibility but are costly to adapt, and implicit neural models that are ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Rubric-Supervised Critic from Sparse Real-World Outcomes
item.description: arXiv:2603.03800v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Academic benchmarks for coding agents tend to reward autonomous task completion, measured by verifiable rewards such as unit-test success. In contrast, real-world coding agents operate with humans in the loop, where success signals are typically noisy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, vision, model, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Biased Generalization in Diffusion Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.03469v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generalization in generative modeling is defined as the ability to learn an underlying distribution from a finite dataset and produce novel samples, with evaluation largely driven by held-out performance and perceived sample quality. In practice, trai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, study, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
